design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate action-platformer stages with run-and-gun mechanics, destroying enemies and obstacles with projectile weapons while managing movement and positioning
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management of weapon energy/ammo while navigating obstacle-heavy level design; balancing forward momentum against environmental hazards
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Armed commando traversing hostile alien environments with advanced weaponry; technical prowess through mastery of movement and combat timing
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Factor 5's technical showcase approach prioritizing sprite scaling, parallax effects, and dense action over narrative; developed as a technically ambitious SNES title emphasizing visual polish and mechanical depth. Cartridge downsizing (6mbit to 4mbit) during development left the game intentionally unfinished, creating a 'rough around edges' identity that later got restored in Director's Cut

difficulty.lives_system: Multi-life system with traditional continue mechanics typical of 16-bit action platformers
difficulty.continues_system: Standard SNES-era checkpoint/continue system allowing level restart after failure
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing; progression saved between level completions
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Hidden level select (accessible via pause menu cheat code: Right, Left, Down, Right, A, Start) allows players to skip to later stages, providing optional difficulty adjustment. Director's Cut variant uses L + R + Select for same function

progression.upgrade_system: Likely weapon/power-up pickup system tied to enemy defeats and stage exploration (typical for Turrican series), though specific mechanics undocumented in sources
progression.unlock_mechanism: Level progression unlocked through stage completion in sequence; hidden level select provides bypass option
replayability_hooks: Hidden level select encourages non-linear revisitation of stages
Sound test access (L + R + X + A at Options > Exit) suggests developer content exploration
Director's Cut (2018) restoration provides alternate version with additional content

world_building_techniques: Enemy/stage design implies hostile alien environment context
Technical visual presentation (parallax, sprite scaling) establishes atmosphere through graphics rather than exposition

boss_design.philosophy: Stage-culminating combat encounters; evidence of planned boss design suggests multi-phase or pattern-based encounters typical of era
notable_bosses: Unused boss theme (BGM 11 in Sound Test) positioned after Stage 3 themes indicates at least one planned Stage 3 boss that was cut during cartridge downsizing. Same theme appears in Mega Turrican final boss and Turrican 3 (Amiga) Stage 3 bosses, suggesting shared boss design philosophy across series

danger_signals: Military/commando protagonist archetype with heavy weaponry
Run-and-gun action framework positioning player as invading force
environmental_storytelling: Level design suggests infiltration/combat missions through alien installations
Technical visual effects (sprite scaling, parallax) create sense of vast, detailed hostile environments

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor: Super Turrican (SNES) development and technical documentation
